failures.explanation: BRM_GA1 double knockout. BRM (SWI/SNF chromatin remodeller) is not represented in the network; the reconciliation mapped only the GA1 component (to GA20OX=0). The model therefore simulates a ga1 single mutant (GA-deficient → late flowering, decreased) but the paper reports the brm ga1 double as flowering earlier (brm KO somehow compensates). Without BRM in the network this cannot be captured.
failures.fix_strategy: Requires adding BRM to the network via Step 1 re-curation; no single REFINEMENT edge-edit will resolve this.

failures.explanation: fkf1 gi combined: both FT activators knocked out simultaneously. Paper reports "unchanged" (both single mutants already delay flowering in SD; double shows no additional loss). Model cascades both KOs multiplicatively through the photoperiod arm, predicting strong FT drop → decreased. The non-additivity of the double mutant is outside the geometric-mean framework.
failures.fix_strategy: Non-additive double mutants are an inherent limit of product-of-inputs models; would require a multiplicative-saturation term in the equation family.

failures.explanation: ft ap2 double KO. ft alone delays flowering (late); ap2 alone accelerates (early). Paper reports "increased" (ap2 dominates). Model: FT=0 removes the florigen cascade while AP2=0 derepresses SOC1 — the FT loss dominates via multiple cascade paths (FT→AP1/LFY/SOC1/FD/FUL), so all three methods predict decreased. Inherent competition between opposing mutants in a cascade.
failures.fix_strategy: Opposing-mutant epistasis is a product-of-cascade limitation. Not fixable without custom per-test logic.

failures.explanation: GI OE under Warm_Temperature. Algebraic/RWR predict increased (more GI → more CO → more FT → earlier flowering), but paper reports decreased (GI OE at warm temps dampens thermoinduction). Missing the PIF4→GI(+1) positive-feedback edge (E201 HIGH, deferred as Judge S22/S24 LOW) would let GI OE properly saturate PIF4. ODE correctly predicts direction, suggesting Hill dynamics handle the saturation.
failures.fix_strategy: Consider adding PIF4 → GI (+1, E201). Would convert GI to non-source with single activator PIF4 and create a safe single-negative feedback with existing GI ⊣ PIF4. Deferred as LOW in Judge iter 3; REFINEMENT can reconsider.

failures.explanation: pif4 mutant(s) under Warm_Temperature. Biology: pif4 blocks warm-temperature-induced flowering → decreased. Model: Warm_Temperature → FT (+1 direct edge E313, added in iter-2 S006d) provides a PIF4-independent path that still promotes FT when PIF4=0. Additionally Warm ⊣ SVP releases a SOC1 inhibitor, further pushing flowering up. The direct Warm→FT edge bypasses PIF4 dependency entirely.
failures.fix_strategy: Option A: remove Warm_Temperature → FT (E313) and route all thermosensing through PIF4 — but this conflicts with H2A.Z / FLM / ELF3 PIF4-independent paths that are genuinely Warm→FT. Option B: accept as framework limit — the network cannot simultaneously model both PIF4-dependent and PIF4-independent warm responses with a single direct edge.

failures.explanation: pif4 mutant(s) under Warm_Temperature. Biology: pif4 blocks warm-temperature-induced flowering → decreased. Model: Warm_Temperature → FT (+1 direct edge E313, added in iter-2 S006d) provides a PIF4-independent path that still promotes FT when PIF4=0. Additionally Warm ⊣ SVP releases a SOC1 inhibitor, further pushing flowering up. The direct Warm→FT edge bypasses PIF4 dependency entirely.
failures.fix_strategy: Option A: remove Warm_Temperature → FT (E313) and route all thermosensing through PIF4 — but this conflicts with H2A.Z / FLM / ELF3 PIF4-independent paths that are genuinely Warm→FT. Option B: accept as framework limit — the network cannot simultaneously model both PIF4-dependent and PIF4-independent warm responses with a single direct edge.

failures.explanation: PIF4 OE under Warm_Temperature. Algebraic and RWR correctly predict increased; only ODE (K=0.5, n=3) predicts decreased. With n=3 and K=0.5 the Hill activation saturates steeply, so PIF4=2 OE overshoots the response curve and feedback from DELLA/GI pushes FT below baseline at this specific (K,n). Not a biology problem — ODE parameter edge-case.
failures.fix_strategy: Consider alternative best-ODE parameters; the ODE sensitivity table shows (K=0.5, n=2) yields 88.2% which is close. No action needed unless ODE is the chosen method.

failures.explanation: soc1 ap2 double KO. soc1 alone delays flowering; ap2 alone accelerates. Paper reports "increased" (ap2 dominates). Model: SOC1 loss cascades through AGL24, LFY, AP1, SVP-reinforcement feedback → broader flowering suppression than the AP2-loss derepression can overcome. Similar failure mode to T077.

svp KO under intermittent cold. Biology: svp mutants are insensitive to intermittent cold (they flower early regardless) → increased. Model: Cold exogenous=1.0 activates VIN3 → represses FLC strongly → FT/SOC1/AP1/LFY are already pushed high at WT+cold baseline; additional SVP KO cannot further elevate the phenotype beyond this ceiling → unchanged. Saturation artefact.
failures.fix_strategy: Reduce cold treatment strength (exogenous=0.5 instead of 1.0) or add bounded-output saturation to the phenotype equation. Not a topology fix.

failures.explanation: tfl1 KO under LD. Algebraic and RWR correctly predict increased (canonical tfl1 phenotype). ODE (K=0.5, n=3) predicts decreased — Hill activation saturates the AP1↔TFL1 bistable switch toward the wrong steady state at this specific parameter set. Not a topology issue.
failures.fix_strategy: Alternative ODE parameters (K=0.5, n=2 gives 88.2%) may resolve. Not a network-level fix.

failures.explanation: TPS1 rescue: otsA-complemented tps1 plants flower at WT age. Reconciliation: TPS1 gm=1.0, comparison_baseline=mutant. Expected=increased (rescued from tps1 lateness). All three methods predict unchanged because with TPS1=1.0 the network simply recomputes to WT baseline values (Flowering_Time=1.0). The comparison to mutant baseline is not actually implemented as (rescued_state / mutant_alone_state) by the validator — ratio=1.0 means the validator compared rescued to WT baseline (also 1.0).
failures.fix_strategy: Rescue-test handling is a validator-level limitation — the baseline=mutant flag is encoded but the validator still ratios against WT steady state. Not a network/refinement problem.

failures.explanation: vin3 KO under vernalization. Biology: vin3 mutants fail to silence FLC even after cold → flowering remains late → decreased. Model: VIN3=0 makes its contribution to FLC inhibition product = 0.01 (activator_floor) → FLC inhibition clamps to min(1/0.01, 10) = 10 (maximum). But with six other autonomous repressors of FLC (FCA, FPA, FLD, FVE, LD + PRC2), the geometric-mean inhibition product dilutes VIN3-specific loss. Under vernalization, baseline WT also has FLC near its floor, and the ratio (vin3+vern)/(WT+vern) comes out near 1.0. Symptom of the FLC §10 inhibitor hard cap preventing VIN3 from being a bottleneck.
failures.fix_strategy: Judge S23 AUTONOMOUS composite restructure: collapse {FCA, FPA, FLD, FVE, LD} into a single AUTONOMOUS node. FLC inhibitor count drops from 7 to 3 (VIN3, PRC2, AUTONOMOUS). VIN3 then becomes a dominant inhibitor whose KO visibly blocks FLC silencing under vernalization. Trade-off: loses per-paralog mutant resolution.
